tier_a.label: Multiple independent sources
tier_a.description: Restaurant recommended by 2+ independent sources, or appears in both ES and EN source pools from different publishers

tier_b.label: Single qualified source
tier_b.description: Restaurant recommended by one named-author source meeting all quality criteria; includes KNOWN_AWARDS entries retained by award-status override

tier_c.label: Auto-rejected
tier_c.description: Failed extraction-time quality checks (quote under 15 words, no specific detail)

pool_notes.en_primary_gap_reason: Structural: Murcia is underserved by English-language restaurant writing. All EN candidates examined and rejected: Emily Luxton (2017, outside YEAR_RANGE), culinarytravels.co.uk (COI: commissioned by Visit Murcia + quotes <15 words), gourmetgorro.blogspot.com (2017), tickereatstheworld.com (2019), alicante-spain.com (2010), thespanishradish.com (2022, no Murcia city restaurants), murciatoday.com (no named author), theculturetrip.com (403 blocked).
pool_notes.concentration_note: 73% of final entries (8/11) are in Murcia Centro — reflects documented dining geography (Part 1: 'dining scene is heavily concentrated in the historic centre around Plaza de las Flores, Calle Trapería, Plaza del Cardenal Belluga and adjacent streets'). Standard 30% concentration cap not applied; geographic justification documented.

sources.author: Miguel Ángel Torralba
sources.note: single-author-confirmed: The Murcialist confirmed as sole-author site via About page, Instagram @themurcialist = 'Miguel Á. Torralba', and gastrobloggers.com listing. Single-Author Site Rule applied — no article-level byline required.
sources.writer_profile: Miguel Ángel Torralba is the sole author and founder of The Murcialist (El GPS gastronómico de Murcia), a personal food blog covering the Murcia restaurant scene. The site is written entirely in Spanish in first person, with reviews of local restaurants across the Murcia municipality.

sources.author: Miguel Ángel Torralba
sources.note: single-author-confirmed: same author as src-murcialist-tandem; Single-Author Site Rule applied.
sources.writer_profile: Miguel Ángel Torralba is the sole author of The Murcialist, a personal food blog focused on the Murcia dining scene. Reviews cover restaurants across all price points in the Murcia municipality.

sources.writer_profile: Miguel Ángel Torralba is the sole author of The Murcialist, a Murcia-focused food blog written in Spanish. Known for reviewing traditional tapas bars and neighbourhood restaurants alongside higher-end venues.

sources.writer_profile: Miguel Ángel Torralba is the sole author of The Murcialist, a personal food guide to Murcia covering the full range from tapas bars to tasting menus.

sources.author: Miguel Ángel Torralba
sources.note: single-author-confirmed: same author as src-murcialist-tandem; Single-Author Site Rule applied.
sources.writer_profile: Miguel Ángel Torralba is the sole author of The Murcialist, a comprehensive food guide to Murcia city covering traditional bars, modern tapas, and chef-led venues.

sources.author: Concha Alcántara
sources.note: explicit byline confirmed in article. Multi-author publication — only articles with explicit Concha Alcántara byline are used. Concha Alcántara is founder and director of The Gastro Times.
sources.writer_profile: Concha Alcántara is the founder and director of The Gastro Times, a Murcia-based gastronomy publication, and host of the Gastroadictos programme on Onda Regional. She covers the Murcia restaurant scene from a local perspective with detailed knowledge of neighbourhood bars and restaurants.

sources.author: José Carlos Capel
sources.note: explicit byline confirmed. José Carlos Capel is creator/president of Madrid Fusión and was El País food critic for 35 years. Rated restaurant 7/10.
sources.writer_profile: José Carlos Capel is one of Spain's most prominent food critics, creator and president of Madrid Fusión, and former food critic of El País for 35 years. His reviews on Gastroactitud carry significant authority in the Spanish restaurant world. His Murcia Local de Ensayo review (December 2024) praises the chef as 'possibly the most technical chef in the Region of Murcia'.

sources.author: Lucía Díaz Madurga
sources.note: National Geographic Viajes is classified secondary per pipeline policy: national publication with national/international audience. Lucía Díaz Madurga is responsible for the gastronomy section of Viajes National Geographic. Article is a Michelin-focused Murcia restaurant guide.
sources.writer_profile: Lucía Díaz Madurga is the gastronomy editor of Viajes National Geographic España, covering restaurant guides and food travel for a national Spanish audience. Her Murcia article focuses on Michelin-recognised restaurants in the city and region.

sources.author: Marina Vega
sources.note: Guía Repsol classified secondary per Part 1 SECONDARY_SOURCES list ('Guía Repsol (national)'). Marina Vega is the credited author of this 'nuestros favoritos' editorial piece.
sources.writer_profile: Marina Vega is a food writer for Guía Repsol's editorial team, contributing 'nuestros favoritos' restaurant features. The Guía Repsol is classified as a secondary source for Murcia as it is a national publication with national audience scope.
